text: The surgeon divides the lino renal ligament using a harmonic device to separate the kidney from the spleen. After identifying the gonadal vein, the colonic mesentery is reflected medially, and the ureterogonadal vein complex is lifted anterior to the swash sheath. Small vessels encountered are clipped and divided with the harmonic. The renal hilum is reached, and the renal vein is identified, dissected, and skeletonized using blunt and sharp dissection. The upper pole is further mobilized within the swast muscle using the harmonic.

The surgeon mobilizes the kidney posteriorly and laterally, completely separating it from the spleen. The hilum is further dissected, with the lumbar vein clipped and divided. The renal artery is identified posterior to the renal vein and dissected using blunt and sharp techniques.

The surgeon incises the peritumoral serotas, fascia, and fat using hook cautery until reaching the renal capsule, delineating the exophytic tumor. The renal artery is further dissected and skeletonized using blunt and sharp dissection. The Da Vinci SI robot is docked, and robotic bulldog clamps are applied to the renal artery and vein separately, allowing for tumor resection in a bloodless field.

The surgeon repairs the opened pelvic lysol system using PDS-IV-O suture, performing internal suturing with V-Loc to address bleeding points. The suture is brought out at the opposite surface of the kidney. Interrupted renal raphe is performed over a surgical bolster, securing hemostasis with Surgiflow. Renal vessels are de-clamped, and hemostasis is reconfirmed. Pirenal fat is sutured over the resected area, and the kidney is pegged to the abdominal wall. A drain is placed, and the specimen is retrieved using an endo-catch bag from a lateral port incision.
